div.head:
211. Letter From President Nixon to Major General GowonSource: National Archives, Nixon Presidential Materials, NSC Files, Presidential Correspondence, 1969–1974, Nigeria-Gen. Gowon. No classification marking.
Nixon responded to Gowonʼs September 18 letter, regretting that a visit was not possible, but holding the invitation open for 1972.
div.p: Thank you for your kind letter of September 18 explaining the urgent concerns in Nigeria and Africa which rule out for the present your undertaking a journey to the United States. I have looked forward to our meeting and genuinely regret its postponement. At the same time, however, I am encouraged by the ongoing success of your Governmentʼs policies at home and the broadening scope of your personal involvement in decisions affecting peace and prosperity in Africa. In these endeavors, you have my continuin support and my warmest wishes for success. I want to assure you that my invitation remains open, and I hope that, as our respective commitments for next year become clearer, it will be possible to find a mutually convenient time for our meeting.
